title: वशीकरण निवारण स्तोत्रम्
devanagariText:
॥ श्री वशीकरण निवारण स्तोत्रम् ॥

ॐ नमो नारायणाय

वशीकरणदोषाणां निवारणार्थमुत्तमम्।
स्तोत्रं प्रवक्ष्यामि शृणु भक्त्या समाहितः॥१॥

ॐ नमो भगवते रुद्राय सर्ववशीकरणनाशनाय।
सर्वमोहविनाशाय सर्वदुष्टप्रमर्दनाय॥२॥

सुदर्शनं महाचक्रं विष्णोः परमायुधम्।
वशीकरणदोषं मे छिन्धि छिन्धि सुदर्शन॥३॥

हनूमान् मारुतिर्वीरो वज्रदेहो महाबलः।
वशीकरणबन्धानां मोचनाय नमो नमः॥४॥

प्रत्यङ्गिरा महादेवी सर्वदुष्टनिवारिणी।
वशीकरणदोषाणां निवारणकरी सदा॥५॥

ॐ ह्रीं क्लीं सर्ववशीकरणदोषनाशनाय नमः।
सर्वमन्त्रं च तन्त्रं च यन्त्रं च विनशिष्यति॥६॥

न मां वशीकरोत्यन्यो न मे मोहो भविष्यति।
सर्वदा मुक्तचित्तोऽहं नारायणकृपाबलात्॥७॥

इदं वशीकरणनिवारणस्तोत्रं यः पठेत्।
सर्ववशीकरणदोषो नश्यति सप्तदिनेन तु॥८॥

॥ इति श्री वशीकरण निवारण स्तोत्रं सम्पूर्णम् ॥
transliteration:
|| Shri Vashikaran Nivaran Stotram ||

Om Namo Narayanaya

Vashikarana-doshanam Nivaranam-artham-uttamam.
Stotram Pravakshyami Shrinu Bhaktya Samahitah.||1||

Om Namo Bhagavate Rudraya Sarva-vashikarana-nashanaya.
Sarva-moha-vinashaya Sarva-dushta-pramardanaya.||2||

Sudarshanam Maha-chakram Vishnoh Parama-ayudham.
Vashikarana-dosham Me Chhindhi Chhindhi Sudarshana.||3||

Hanuman Marutir-viro Vajra-deho Maha-balah.
Vashikarana-bandhanam Mochanaya Namo Namah.||4||

Pratyangira Maha-devi Sarva-dushta-nivarini.
Vashikarana-doshanam Nivarana-kari Sada.||5||

Om Hrim Klim Sarva-vashikarana-dosha-nashanaya Namah.
Sarva-mantram Cha Tantram Cha Yantram Cha Vinashishyati.||6||

Na Mam Vashikaroty-anyo Na Me Moho Bhavishyati.
Sarvada Mukta-chitto-aham Narayana-kripa-balat.||7||

Idam Vashikarana-nivarana-stotram Yah Pathet.
Sarva-vashikarana-dosho Nashyati Sapta-dinena Tu.||8||

|| Iti Shri Vashikaran Nivaran Stotram Sampurnam ||
hindiMeaning: वशीकरणदोष निवारण हेतु यह उत्तम स्तोत्र है। भगवान् रुद्र को नमन जो सर्ववशीकरणनाशक, सर्वमोहविनाशक और सर्वदुष्टप्रमर्दक हैं। सुदर्शन चक्र मेरे वशीकरणदोष को काटो। हनुमान वज्रदेही महाबली वशीकरणबन्धन से मुक्त करें। प्रत्यंगिरा महादेवी सर्वदुष्ट निवारिणी वशीकरणदोष निवारण करें। कोई मुझे वशीकरण नहीं कर सकता, नारायणकृपा बल से मैं सदा मुक्तचित्त हूँ। सात दिन पाठ करने से सर्ववशीकरणदोष नष्ट होता है।
benefits: Powerful stotra to neutralize vashikaran and black magic effects
Invokes Rudra, Sudarshana, Hanuman, and Pratyangira for protection
Traditionally recited for removal of all mantra, tantra, and yantra directed against the devotee
Frees the mind from external control and hypnotic influence
Seven days of recitation removes all vashikaran dosha
Invokes mental freedom and clarity through narayana's grace
description: Dedicated to Lord Vishnu, the Vashikaran Nivaran Stotram (वशीकरण निवारण स्तोत्रम्) is a devotional hymn of praise revered in Hindu tradition. Drawn from Tantra Shastra, this text has been cherished by devotees for centuries. Devotees recite this prayer to destroy all mantra, tantra, and yantra directed against the devotee and free the mind from external control and hypnotic influence. It is also said to grant mental freedom and clarity through Narayana's grace. This prayer is traditionally recited on Saturday (Shanivar) and Tuesday (Mangalvar) to invoke divine grace. Comprising 8 verses, it can be completed in approximately 4 minutes, making it suitable for daily devotional practice. On this page, you can read the complete Vashikaran Nivaran Stotram in Devanagari Sanskrit with English transliteration, Hindi meaning (arth), and free PDF download for offline recitation.
seoDescription: Read Vashikaran Nivaran Stotram in Sanskrit with Hindi meaning. Powerful prayer to remove vashikaran, black magic, and mental control effects.
relatedSearches: Vashikaran Nivaran Stotram lyrics in Sanskrit Hindi
Tantric protection from black magic stotram
Hanuman Bajrang Baan kavach against tantra
Vashikaran nivaran puja vidhi at home

seoFaqs.question: What does Vashikaran Nivaran Stotram aim to achieve?
seoFaqs.answer: Vashikaran Nivaran Stotram is a category of protective Tantric prayer composed to neutralise vashikaran, abhichara and maraNa-prayoga, that is, attempts at psychic control, sorcery and life-targeting magic directed at the devotee. The hymn invokes deities recognised in the Tantric tradition as masters of counter-magic, primarily Bagalamukhi, Pratyangira, Sudarshana, Narasimha and Mahakali, asking them to bind the tongue, the will and the instruments of the perpetrator and return the harmful energy to its source. Unlike attack-mantras, nivaran prayers are framed defensively: the devotee seeks shelter (sharan) and asks the deity to function as a kavacha or armor around the body and home. (Sources: Astromantra Varahi Nigraha Ashtakam; ManBlunder Sri Vasya Varahi.)

seoFaqs.question: Which deities are most commonly invoked in counter-vashikaran liturgy?
seoFaqs.answer: Five deities dominate the counter-vashikaran tradition. Bagalamukhi, the eighth Mahavidya, is invoked specifically to stambhana, that is, paralyse the speech and action of the adversary. Pratyangira-devi, depicted with a lion's face, returns sent magic back to the sender, and her stotram is the principal anti-abhichara hymn in South Indian temples. Sudarshana of Vishnu is invoked through the Sudarshana Ashtakam to slice through psychic bondage. Narasimha through the Narasimha Kavacham guards against unseen enemies and night-time attacks. Mahakali through the Karunashtakam and Pratyangira-Mahakali stotras provides the cremation-ground protective force. Vashikaran Nivaran Stotram texts in circulation typically draw on this same theological pool. (Sources: Astromantra; ManBlunder Sri Vasya Varahi Mahamantra.)

seoFaqs.question: Are these texts considered safe for daily home recitation?
seoFaqs.answer: Counter-vashikaran prayers framed purely as protective shelter (sharan) are widely regarded as safe for daily home recitation, particularly the more public hymns such as Sudarshana Ashtakam, Hanuman Bajrang Baan, Narasimha Kavacham and Devi Kavacham. Strictly Tantric forms such as the Bagalamukhi Mala-mantra, Pratyangira homa-mantras and the more aggressive nivaran-prayogas of the Damar tantra and Uddamareshvara tantra traditionally require initiation (diksha) from a qualified guru, observance of purity rules, and often dedicated yantra-puja, since they involve invoking pratikriya energies. Devotees should distinguish carefully between the sharan-stotram form and the prayoga form before adopting daily practice. (Sources: Exotic India Vashikaran Totake; ManBlunder.)

seoFaqs.question: What is the relationship between Vashikaran Nivaran Stotram and Bagalamukhi worship?
seoFaqs.answer: Bagalamukhi is the Mahavidya most closely identified with stambhana-vidya, the paralysing power that freezes the speech, motion and intention of an adversary. Many Vashikaran Nivaran Stotrams in print are extracted from or modelled on the Bagalamukhi Kavacham, the Bagalamukhi Stotram from the Mantra Maharnava, and the Bagalamukhi Hridaya. Practitioners distinguish between the nivaran intent, that is, neutralising vashikaran cast on oneself, and the offensive stambhana intent of paralysing an opponent's success; the latter is forbidden for personal vendetta in mainstream guru-lineages. Devotees commonly substitute the Pitambari-devi yellow lamp and yellow cloth puja during Vashikaran Nivaran practice. (Sources: Astromantra; ManBlunder.)

seoFaqs.question: Are there scriptural alternatives if one cannot find the specific Vashikaran Nivaran Stotram?
seoFaqs.answer: Yes. The Sudarshana Ashtakam by Vedanta Desika, the Narasimha Kavacham from the Brahmanda Purana, Hanuman's Bajrang Baan and Sankat Mochan Stotram, the Devi Kavacham from the Devi Mahatmyam, and Adi Shankara's Lakshmi-Narasimha Karavalamba Stotram are all considered effective substitutes within mainstream Vaishnava and Shakta practice. These hymns offer comprehensive protection against unseen enemies, malefic mantra-prayoga, ghost possession and abhichara, and require no Tantric initiation. Many Hindu households maintain a daily recitation of the Hanuman Chalisa and Sudarshana Ashtakam as a baseline defence against vashikaran and similar negative influences. (Sources: ManBlunder; Astromantra; Exotic India.)

seoFaqs.question: What lifestyle and ritual precautions accompany Vashikaran Nivaran practice?
seoFaqs.answer: Traditional teachers recommend that anyone undertaking serious nivaran-sadhana observe vegetarian diet, maintain brahmacharya during the practice period, avoid alcohol and onion-garlic, and recite the chosen stotram with bath-purity at sunrise or after dusk. The practitioner is advised against publicly naming the suspected sender of vashikaran or engaging in counter-aggression; the entire framework is reframed from revenge to surrender, in which the deity assumes responsibility for justice. Many practitioners also wear a Tulsi or Rudraksha mala, install a small kavacha-yantra in the puja room, and offer haldi-water or saffron-water arghya to the chosen deity through the practice cycle of typically 41 or 108 days. (Sources: Astromantra; Exotic India; ManBlunder.)
